articles.title: Specification-driven composition for flexible data workflows
articles.excerpt: Specification-driven composition addresses a common scalability bottleneck in data pipelines. Data pipelines often start as simple scripts, but as they grow, you duplicate transformation logic and small changes cascade across multiple workflows.

articles.title: OpenAI’s GPT-5.6 is now live
articles.excerpt: As expected, OpenAI on Thursday made its family of GPT-5.6 models available to its app and API users globally.

articles.title: Safely run AI-generated code in Cloud Run sandboxes
articles.excerpt: Here’s a question we hear often at Google Cloud: How do you safely run AI-generated code or untrusted binaries without putting your host application, data, and cloud credentials at risk?

articles.title: How GitHub gave every repository a durable owner
articles.excerpt: GitHub had over 14,000 repositories. Fewer than half had clear ownership.

articles.title: Migrate Amazon EC2 to EKS Auto Mode using Kiro CLI and MCP servers
articles.excerpt: In this post, you walk through a practical migration scenario where a Node.js web application running on EC2 instances is migrated into a highly scalable, containerized service on EKS Auto Mode.

articles.title: Solve harder problems with AlphaEvolve, now available to everyone on Google Cloud
articles.excerpt: Many of the most challenging and valuable problems in the world are related to optimization. Now, AI is now making these problems tractable.

articles.title: Business intelligence plugins for Grafana: A support update
articles.excerpt: In January, we announced that Grafana Labs had assumed maintenance of the business intelligence (BI) plugins created by Volkov Labs, and committed to a six-month maintenance period. Today, we’re sharing an update: we're extending our maintenance commitment through the end of 2026.

articles.title: Why we cannot wait for better post-quantum signature algorithms
articles.excerpt: NIST is advancing nine new post-quantum signature algorithms as potential candidates for future standardization. We take a closer look at all of them, and argue that while they are in the works and show great potential, we should use ML-DSA for now — the best one currently available.

articles.title: GPT-5.6 is now the preferred model in Microsoft 365 Copilot
articles.excerpt: Learn how GPT-5.6 powers Microsoft 365 Copilot with stronger AI capabilities across Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Chat, and Cowork for faster, higher-quality work.

articles.title: ChatGPT is now a partner for your most ambitious work
articles.excerpt: ChatGPT Work is an agent that can take action across your apps and files, stay with a project for hours if needed, and turn a goal into finished work.

articles.title: Autopilot Clusters with GKE managed DRANET: GPUs and TPUs
articles.excerpt: Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) managed DRANET supports both GPUs and TPUs. There are several configurations to use this implementation, including standard cluster (where you have full control) and autopilot cluster (where Google does the heavy configs for you).

articles.title: Introducing Red Hat OpenShift Service Mesh 3.4
articles.excerpt: Red Hat OpenShift Service Mesh 3.4 is generally available with Red Hat OpenShift and Red Hat OpenShift Platform Plus.

articles.title: From automatic CI/CD to autonomous agentic workflows: Continuous AI with Red Hat OpenShift
articles.excerpt: You’ve been asked to adopt AI tools with a promise they will accelerate your time to production and improve the quality of your code. Along the way, you’ve noticed a huge increase in the number of changes submitted to code and you’ve also likely felt the pain of your systems and processes not being able to keep up.

articles.title: Green DevOps: Why carbon measurement belongs in your CI/CD pipeline
articles.excerpt: A typical software team runs hundreds of CI/CD jobs a day. Each one runs on compute and burns energy that doesn't show up in your pipeline logs, including its carbon impact.

articles.title: Symlinks Are Still Scary (And Yes, You Can Commit Them to Git)
articles.excerpt: A harmless-looking symlink in a Git repo can redirect a tool into reading or writing anywhere on your machine. That old trick is now showing up in AI coding assistants, with nasty results.
